title: 30o: BlobLookupResponse.not_found type confusing
description: Location: crates/jmap-chat/src/methods/blob.rs line 33. not_found is typed Option<Vec<String>> with #[serde(default)]. With serde(default), an absent field deserializes to None. But the spec says absent and empty-array mean the same thing (no missing blobs). The Option wrapper forces every caller to write .as_deref().unwrap_or(&[]) as seen in test 57 (methods_test.rs line 2493). Compare ChangesResponse.destroyed which is Vec<String> (no Option) — absent JSON array becomes empty vec via serde(default). Why burden: a solo maintainer will forget the None-vs-empty distinction and write code that treats None and Some(vec![]) differently when they are semantically identical. Suggested fix: change to Vec<String> with #[serde(default)], matching the pattern used elsewhere in the codebase.
close_reason: fixed: changed to Vec<String> with serde(default)
